title: Alerting support for queries using template variables
body:
It would be pretty useful if grafana would support alerting for queries using template variables. The way I see it work it would be as follows:

1. Generate queries foreach template variable combination (discarding template variable for __all__) 
2. When generating queries, consider the frozen list if the template variable is set to never refresh, else update the template variable list
3. Allow filtering (trough regex or by providing a static value) for each template variable

The current workaround is to use an invisible wildcard metric, but the problem I see with this approach is that it loses context.

title: Hide empty / no data panels
body:
Use case: 
Server A have eth0, eth1 and server B have eth0 and ppp0.

I create a templated variable $netInterface and use the query "tag_values(os.net.bytes,iface)" in it, so it will get the possible values "eth0, eth1, ppp0". 
When using this variable with the repeated panel feature, I'll get 3 panels (one for each value), but in server A, the ppp0 panel will be empty, and on server B eth1 panel will be empty. If I could configure Grafana to suppress the empty ones (just like I can suppress empty series' legend), the dashboard would be much easier to interpret.

title: [Feature request] Multiple alerts per graph
body:
As per http://docs.grafana.org/alerting/rules/, Grafana plans to track state per series in future releases.
- "If a query returns multiple series then the aggregation function and threshold check will be evaluated for each series. What Grafana does not do currently is track alert rule state per series." and 
- "To improve support for queries that return multiple series we plan to track state per series in a future release"

But it seems like there can be use cases where we have graphs containing set of metrics for which different sets of alerts are required. This is slightly different from "Support per series state change" ( https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/6041 ) because
1.  The action (notifications) can be different. 
2. Also, tracking separate states of an alert is not always preferred (as the end-user would need to know the details behind the individual states ) vs just knowing if alert is triggered.


Grafana version = 4.x

title: Allow custom mapping of template variable value -> display text
[Feat] Conditional formatting
body:
## Introduction

As of writing (Grafana v5.1-pre) we have support for 
* coloring background/value in the Singlestat panel based on a numeric range and 3 colors
* coloring cell/value/row in the Table panel based on a numeric range and 3 colors

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1668778/38026938-4fea44ae-328e-11e8-9417-ad441512b1c4.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1668778/38026945-5346b150-328e-11e8-95d7-1a76082a5519.png)

There are a lot of feature requests/issues requesting greater support of coloring in table panel and the singlestat panel:
* #7631 [Feature request] Allow Text to Value mappings in Singlestat panel
* #3601 table panel: colorize cell value based on regex pattern match
* #9500 Feature request: Allow thresholds for Time column in tables
* #3608 Thresholds with more than 3 comma-separated values
* #8404 [Feature request] Singlestat null value color 
* #4821 Table Panel coloring shouldn't require display of referenced column
* #9709 Single stat panel with gradient color
* #7411 [Feature request] Variable threshold values in Singlestats panel
* #7286 [Feature request] Row and/or Column styles in Table

Historically we've received a few PR's that tried to solve some of the issues above, but we haven't been able to merge them because we feel the solutions are either hard to understand from a user perspective or either it makes the Grafana code base/features more and more shattered.

## Proposal

This issue aims to find one solution for solving all (or most) of the issues above and by that delivering a good and consistent user experience. By that said, we probably want to split this up in smaller issues if we decide that this is a good way forward.

In general the *Conditions editor* for alerts are very similar to a rule/conditional formatting editor functionality and would probably fit quite nice with a few modifications. This is something to take into considerations when implementing this.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1668778/38030188-3779b9f4-3298-11e8-92f8-b2da14b54be3.png)

### Conditional rules evaluator

A panel needing conditional formatting shouldn't need to implement their own solution for evaluating conditional rules thus making this a core component/helper library is a must. 
It would be nice if this component also could return a list of rule types that it supports.

Examples of rule types:
* Value is empty
* Value is not empty
* Text contains
* Text does not contain
* Text starts with
* Text ends with
* Text is exactly
* Date is
* Date is before
* Date is after
* Greater than
* Greater than or equal to
* Less than
* Less than or equal to
* Is equal to
* Is not equal to
* Is between
* Is not between

### Singlestat panel - colorize background/value

Changes here compared to current functionality is that 
* it allow you to define unlimited amount of color formatting's compared to today's maximum of three.

title: Feature Request: template variable string manipulations
body:
It would be nice if Grafana template variables could have some string manipulations applied. This could be useful to be able to refer to data about the same thing from various datasources from a variable, where that thing is named slightly differently for technical reasons. For instance, if you have a website that you refer to by the name "some_site" in your systems, but you have an AWS S3 bucket for it where you can't use underscores, you could transform that name to 'some-site', or 'somesite' instead.

The immediately obvious ideas for what kind of manipulations to support would be the various variable substitutions that bash supports. In bash I would do the above example by using `${var//_/-}` or `${var//_}`.

title: Localize time_format in graphs
body:
Hello dear developers team.
Thank you for a awesome product, but I have one problem.

Now there is a hadcoded US date format in [time_format function](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/master/src/app/panels/graph/graph.js#L373).
The most annoying case is display month and day. When I see something like "2/3" I'm a bit confused. Is it "the second of Mart" or "the third of February"?
The most sad thing, that I can't to configure this behaviour. 

Unfortunately the simplest way (and may be the most proper) doesn't help here. I mean [toLocaleString](https://developer.mozilla.org/en/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number/toLocaleString) with additional options. You can return different array of options instead of hardcoded format pattern and this method convert date in accordance with a right locale.
But in our case there is a jquery plot and it requires date format for converting timestamp by itself.

So, the second way is make some kind of mapping locale -> format array. [Example](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2388115/get-locale-short-date-format-using-javascript/9893752#9893752). It seems a bit ugly. But it could be a single working solution.

May be I missed some obvious and better solutions. That's why I didn't create a pull request. =)

title: Alerting: Support per series state change tracking for queries that return multiple series
body:
Currently, we don't update the alert message if the alert query return new/other series then the first one. 

Let's say that one alert executes and have an eval match on "serie 1".
Next time the alert executes "serie 1" is fine but "serie 2" is alerting.  
This will go unnoticed in our current implementation. 

When an alert is triggered we should create a key based on the alerting series. The next time the same alert is triggered we can compare the state and the key created by the series.

If the data source support dimensions, we should include those when creating the key. 

One suggestions for how to implement such key creation:
ex
```
{ "serie 1", datapoints: ... },
{ "serie 2", datapoints: ... }
```
Should become `serie 1;serie 2`

```
{ "web-front-01", tags: {"server": "web-front-01", "application": "web"}, datapoints: ... },
{ "web-front-01", tags: {"server": "web-front-02", "application": "web"}, datapoints: ... }
```
Should become `web-front-01{server=web-front-01,application=web};web-front-02{server=web-front-02,application=web}`

Keys might seem long but I don't think it matters that much. Perhaps we could find a way of shortening them by just storing a hash of it or something like that.

title: [Feature Request] Support AWS SigV4 authentication for Elasticsearch Datasource
body:
I would like to use an [Amazon Elasticsearch Service](https://aws.amazon.com/elasticsearch-service/) cluster as an Elasticsearch datasource in Grafana. This works just fine if I allow public access to the cluster, but I'd prefer to lock it down to authenticated users only.

The only user-level access control Amazon ES supports is [through AWS SigV4 signing the HTTP requests to Elasticsearch with IAM credentials](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticsearch-service/latest/developerguide/es-createupdatedomains.html#es-createdomain-configure-access-policies). For example, [here's how the Golang `elastic` client does it](https://github.com/olivere/elastic/wiki/Using-with-AWS-Elasticsearch-Service).

I had a look at how the `proxy` mode of the ElasticSearch datasource works, and it looks like it would be pretty straightforward to build this signing into `dataproxy.go`, similarly to [how it's handled for the CloudWatch data source.](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/master/pkg/api/dataproxy.go#L81-L84)

title: [New Data Source]: VictoriaMetrics
body:
### Background

VictoriaMetrics is a time series database. It can be used for collecting and processing infrastructure metrics, k8s metrics, APM, etc. It is compatible with Prometheus exposition format and protocols.
VictoriaMetrics is open source and distributed under Apache 2.0 License - see https://github.com/VictoriaMetrics/VictoriaMetrics 

### Use case

VictoriaMetrics is widely used in open-source. Especially, in integration with Grafana.
Many users use Prometheus datasource to connect to VictoriaMetrics for querying data in Grafana.
However, VictoriaMetrics provides own datasource plugin - https://github.com/VictoriaMetrics/victoriametrics-datasource

This plugin is open-source and publicly available for everyone. It is developed and supported by VictoriaMetrics maintainers and community members and does not require development efforts from the Grafana team. It would be great to have this plugin signed to make it easier for users to install. Currently, users need to change Grafana's security settings in order to install the plugin.

### Contribution

title: Alert notification silencing
body:
_We are not really sure about how to design the silencing part of alerting. But our intial take is something like this._

Silencing alerts means that no notifications regarding state changes will be sent. The Alerts will still execute and save state changes to the database. Silence rules should have an explicit end time to avoid silencing alerts for ever by mistake. It should be possible to have recurring silencing rules to be able to silence certain alerts during nights etc. 

It should be possible to silence by
- Alert Id
- Dashboard Id
- Notification channel
- Alert name pattern
- Alert eval match name pattern

Alert silence rules are always temporary, IE they have a TTL

title: Loki: Support log volume histogram in custom dashboards
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/grafana/grafana/discussions/46600
Originally posted by **dvidben** March 15, 2022 In the Loki explore view, grafana offers a histogram to show the log volume in a histogram supporting following features: - log aggregations over time - split on log level. - log levels color palette described here: https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/explore/logs-integration/ (section titled "Log level"). However, current available charts in grafana do not offer the same capabilities to create a dashboard with the same features: 1. Bucketing based on time is limited in other charts (i.e: histogram, heatmap) 2. Log-level based color palette is not available (time series using bars - color palette is available for values but not legends, histogram, heatmap) This request is to support adding a log-volume histogram with the same capabilities in custom dashboards (either by adding a specific new control or extending the current capabilities in the timeseries or histogram charts)

title: [FeatureRequest] Dashboards: Add real time share instead of snapshot
body:
Snapshots are great to provide a "snapshot" of a dashboard. But with the current implementation, sharing dashboards e.g. with a limited set of key customers or key partners requires regularly generate new up-to-date dashboard snapshots to include the data of the recent days.

**Grafana should have a way of creating realtime shares with live data**: The shared dashboard should have no time limit and query the data live from the datasource. Of course this only works only on datasources that have server access mode enabled to make request from the Grafana server directly. So there is no need to expose the query to the user/browser, as the Grafana server knows which queries are "shared" and which are not shared (no leak of possible sensitive data).

By sharing a read-only dashboard with an external user it would be even more ideal to create also Grafana-users that have read-only access of selected "shared" dashboard.


The following (closed and not implemented) issues also requested that use-case, but didn't show a solution as explained above to the "authentication problem": 
 * https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/2133: I don't see a security issue or implementation issue like discussed: The permissioning should happen inside the Grafana-server and not inside a Datasource. The query should also made from the Grafana-server, the client/browser never has to receive the query. The only thing the client can change is the time-interval.
 * https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/12031 (closed feature request without discussion about implementation)
 * https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/5547: @torkelo there stated "data is actually requested from the data source then the user will have to access the data source". I disagree with it, the data can be also be requested by the Grafana-server, the only parameter the user can change is the start/end time of the query.

Background: I have logs from a bunch of smaller job pods, they are more natural to be read in chronological order, that is from oldest at the top to newest at the bottom, just like the logs would read directly on the filesystem.

title: Support for self-hosted Git servers in Git sync
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**Why is this needed**:

The new Git Sync feature is awesome — being able to version control dashboards is a game-changer.
However, it's currently limited to GitHub.com, which makes it unusable for teams that host their own Git servers.

In my case, we use a self-hosted Gitea instance within our company's infrastructure. It would be great to sync dashboards directly with it instead of relying on a third-party service like GitHub.


**What would you like to be added**:

Support for generic Git servers — ideally with the ability to set a custom Git endpoint (HTTP(S) or SSH), along with authentication methods like SSH keys, tokens, or basic auth.

This could make Git Sync backend-agnostic and open it up to all kinds of setups (Gitea, GitLab self-hosted, Bitbucket Server, etc.).


**Who is this feature for?**

Any team or user running their own Git infrastructure — whether for security, compliance, or just more control over their tooling.
Specifically: DevOps teams, platform engineers, and Grafana power users in enterprise or private environments.

title: Dashboard workflow for provisioned dashboards
body:
Grafana currently allows operators to load dashboard JSON files from the filesystem into Grafana using provisioning. The user can edit the dashboard in the browser but cannot save it. To update the dashboard the user has to copy the edited JSON to the files themselves. 

One idea that we have is to allow Grafana to…

1. Provision dashboards directly from Github 
2. Create pull requests within Grafana
3. Review pull requests within Grafana by rendering dashboards from pull requests.

Questions:

- Would you like to use #1, #2 and #3 or just one of them?
- Would you have dedicated repositories for dashboards or mix dashboards within other repositories?
- What source code versioning system are you using in your organization? Github? Gitlab? Bitbucket? Stand-alone git server?

There are still many things to consider. So if you are interested in such a feature. Please reach out to us! We want to talk more with you! :) Either in this issue or over mail carl@grafana.com

body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**What would you like to be added**:
 
When creating new folders / dashboards, the following permissions are set by default:

- Admin: Admin
- Editor:  Edit
- Viewer: View

However, these permissions applied on creation are currently not configurable (only **after** creation). In some cases, it might not be desired to grant View access to **all** viewers by default.

title: New Multi-Stat Panel
body:
Use case to be able show many stats or small graphs in one panel. Singlestat panel is very inefficient as each panel issues it's own queries.

## Multi stat panel basic requirements 
1) Support horizontal and vertical layouts
2) Support different layout configurations 
3) Support bars (horizontal gauge and thresholds) 
4) Support small graphs 
5) Maybe a trend option (to show percent up/down)

title: [Feature Request] Support Import/Export of Datasources as JSON Files
body:
# Motivation

To be able to automate Grafana deployments and migrations, especially on Docker.
# Problem

The import/export mechanism in Grafana 3.1 allows you to parameterize the datasource, via the `__input` section of the JSON file. However, it's a still manual step to configure and select the datasource.
# Solution

Provide support for the import and export of datasources as JSON files. This should also support loading datasource JSON files from the filesystem. This could be configured as a `[datasources.json]`,  similar to the `[dashboards.json]` mechanism.
# Advantages
1. This would help facilitate Docker deployments, as automated container deployment becomes much simpler if all required configuration is available on the filesystem (as opposed to an interaction with the UI or the REST API, which requires somewhat complex interactions).
2. Exported datasource configuration files could be put in version control systems such as Git.

title: Make it possible to provision users
body:
**What would you like to be added**:

Add a possibility to provision orgs and users with passwords and permissions, not just the super-admin, via configuration.

**Why is this needed**:

In this deployment context:
- grafana deployed from a continuous integration pipeline
- grafana setup spanning multiple organizations
- grafana deployed behind an authentication/authorization proxy

It is labor intensive and error prone to replicate all the org and user management performed in whatever directory system the auth(n|z) proxy uses in grafana.

Simplest thing to do is to be able to provision a structure of orgs and users inside grafana with one user provided with desired combinations of permissions, by org, have the proxy add a corresponding basic auth header to proxied requests, and have grafana only ever know about these provisioned orgs and users.

In such a scenario, everything in grafana will be read-only, since the CI pipeline should be the one controlling everything that gets deployed, including dashboards and data sources, so there is no point in using specific users for access to track changes - there simply won't be any changes.

Right now, the only way to do this is by having the CI pipeline configure everything via the HTTP API, after grafana was redeployed and started. This is cumbersome.

title: Formatting Currency without abbreviation
body:
<!--
Please use this template to create your bug report. By providing as much info as possible you help us understand the issue, reproduce it and resolve it for you quicker. Therefor take a couple of extra minutes to make sure you have provided all info needed.

PROTIP: record your screen and attach it as a gif to showcase the issue.

- Questions should be posted to: https://community.grafana.com
- Use query inspector to troubleshoot issues: https://bit.ly/2XNF6YS
- How to record and attach gif: https://bit.ly/2Mi8T6K
-->

**What happened**:
Wanted to output a currency value as the full value, without abbreviation, a currency symbol and thousand commas for readability. 

**What you expected to happen**:
To get something like $53,456.75 instead of $53.46K or $53456.75 or 53,456.75

**How to reproduce it (as minimally and precisely as possible)**:
Set a values units in the following ways:

* Currency / Dollar ($) - this gives you the abbreviated version which is no good for reporting.
* prefix:$ - this gives you an unabbreviated version but without the commas, making large numbers hard to read.
* Misc / Locale Format - this gives you the unabbreviated version with the commas but no currency sign.

None of these get the formatting required.

**Anything else we need to know?**:
I would suggest there are couple of options to fix this, either:

* Add "prefix" as a separate option for a value so it can have both a "prefix" and a unit type attached to it. In this way we could prefix it with $ and set the unit to "Misc / Locale Format".

OR 

* Add an extra option to the "Currency" unit option to allow for the abbreviation to be turned off.

I think the first of these offers the most flexibility but the second is more intuitive.

Forum post: https://community.grafana.com/t/formatting-currency-without-abbreviation/50287

**Environment**:
- Grafana version: 8.0.5
- Data source type & version: MySQL 8.0.19
- OS Grafana is installed on: Docker
- User OS & Browser: Mac / Chrome
- Grafana plugins: None

title: [Feature request] JWT Authentication
body:
By now it would be great to get JWT Authentication support.

One use case for this would be Google IAP (https://cloud.google.com/iap/). In this case Google would do the authentication and passes a JWT Header containing the user information. I guess Google is just the starter for such use cases and other implementations might evolve soon.

There is still the option for OAuth, but this would be redundant for such cases, as OAuth takes already place at the auth proxy and we merely need to parse the passed header. There's also no issue from the security perspective, as the `X-Goog-Authenticated-User-JWT` header is signed. 

Here are the details about their respective headers:
- https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/identity-howto#getting_the_users_identity_in_compute_name_short_container_name_short_and_gae_name_short_flexible_environment
- https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/signed-headers-howto

This is in response to #1247

title: [Feature Request] Add support for performing math across multiple fields to InfluxDB query builder
body:
Currently, Grafana (2.6) supports math functionality with the field on the left side within the InfluxDB query builder. I.e. it can build queries like

```
SELECT mean("field") / 100 AS "field" FROM "measurement" WHERE "tag" = 'value' AND $timeFilter GROUP BY time($interval) fill(null)
```

It would be nice to also have support for math with fields on both sides, for example something like

```
SELECT mean("field1") + mean("field2") + mean("field3") AS "field" FROM "measurement" WHERE "tag" = 'value' AND $timeFilter GROUP BY time($interval) fill(null)
```

My suggestions: - If the default template produces markdown, then the default `parse_mode` should be set to `MarkdownV2`. - Telegram `parse_mode` param should be made configurable in the contact point setting. **Environment**: - Grafana version: docker.io/grafana/grafana:8.5.2 arm64 build

title: Configuring Grafana for Active Directory LDAP authentication
body:
I'd like to request an update to the LDAP configuration at http://docs.grafana.org/installation/ldap/. It took me quite some time to configure Grafana in a decent way for Active Directory and the referenced documentation and other sources on the Internet weren't of much help. Specifically it suggests you need to have a read only user and its password stored in the `ldap.toml`. This is not ideal, nor necessary.

Anyway, what I found.

Assuming:

* IP address of the AD server: `10.0.0.1`;
* AD domain: `CORP`;
* AD DNS name: `corp.local`.

Then, the following configuration will work.

```yaml
[[servers]]
host = "10.0.0.1"
port = 3269
use_ssl = true
start_tls = false
ssl_skip_verify = true
bind_dn = "CORP\\%s"
search_filter = "(sAMAccountName=%s)"
search_base_dns = ["dc=corp,dc=local"]

[servers.attributes]
name = "givenName"
surname = "sn"
username = "sAMAccountName"
member_of = "memberOf"
email =  "mail"
```

This configuration specifically works around what the documentation describes as how you should configure your `bind_dn`. By including the AD domain in the `bind_dn`, you can leave the `bind_password` and the bind will be done on the username and password. This is Active Directory specific and likely won't work in OpenLDAP (see https://serverfault.com/a/643615). The only thing you then need is the `search_filter` which in the above example searches on username, and the `search_base_dns` which has the expanded DNS name form.

The port uses a default, encrypted, port. There are a few options for this, and https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd772723(v=ws.10).aspx has the details.

And that's it. You still need to configure `[[servers.group_mappings]]` in a decent manner, but that should be it. Also, there are still a few more options in e.g. to provide a certificate (and `ssl_skip_verify = true` is not ideal). Review and modify as necessary.

title: Make Grafana more friendly to read-only MySQL database
body:
### What Grafana version are you using?
5.2.2
### What datasource are you using?
Mixed (irrelevant to my question)
### What OS are you running grafana on?
Linux
### What did you do?
Grafana is one of the main tools for troubleshooting during outages and we are looking to a way to make it as much reliable as possible.
We have a big installation of Grafana (around 90 instances in the same Domain/Org) 
We are using MySQL to store both Grafana config and Sessions.
We are using MySQL Master->many_Slaves replication chain managed by Orchestrator https://github.com/github/orchestrator 
Since Grafana can utilize only single MySQL server(Master) for it internal needs #399 #3676 we are providing service discovery by using short living DNS records pointing to a current Master, updated by Orchestrator for redundancy. This setup work fine until recently.
The recent DNS outage proved that this DNS dependency is better to be avoided, as If DNS fails then we do not have a way to discover MySQL and Grafana fails to perform any operations without MySQL available. Essentially if your DNS is broken, then everything else is broken.

We were thinking about adding some extra fail-over logic on Grafana boxes where we could pull all available slaves and make them available locally for grafana, for instance via /etc/hosts. This should make grafana available for troubleshooting, however grafana just can't deal with read-only database.

### What was the expected result?
I would expect it to keep providing read-only access to all dashboards, search and navigation should work.

### What happened instead?
 If restarted it can't start 
t=2018-09-24T13:43:23+0200 lvl=eror msg="Server shutdown" logger=server reason="Service init failed: Datasource provisioning error: Error 1290: The MySQL server is running with the --read-only option so it cannot execute this statement"

 If IP of the MySQL has been changed to RO then it can't search for dashboards and navigation is impossible with "Server error" or "Unknown error"

 Proper solution as I can see it: 
- Teach Grafana to work with Write / RO connections separately
- Fail gracefully if Write connection unavailable

title: Alerting: Disable (...) is allowed to be saved on top of other parameters but API gives bad request data updating policies
body:
### What happened?

There are 2 main issues:
1. alertingSimplifiedRouting UI allows to save an alert with the `Disable (...)` grouping over the grafana_folder and alertname ones. After that, you are not able to edit notification policies receiving the error ([video 1](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/assets/122215118/b791ce75-eab3-489b-ba50-3682c66c4967)):
`level=error msg="bad request data" error="cannot have wildcard group_by (...) and other other labels at the same time"`
2. alertingSimplifiedRouting UI doesn't allow to set the `Disable (...)` grouping option alone without other options ([video 2](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/assets/122215118/9b26d3dc-bf9b-42c3-9974-cfe3e91476c5)). However, you can set this through API like this:
```
    "notification_settings": {
        "receiver": "contact-point-name",
        "group_by": [
            "..."
        ]
    }
```



### What did you expect to happen?

1. Don't allow to save an alert with the `Disable (...)` grouping over the grafana_folder and alertname ones.
2. Fix alertingSimplifiedRouting UI to allow setting the `Disable (...)` grouping option alone without other options.

### Did this work before?

This is a new feature in 10.4

### How do we reproduce it?

1. create any alert with Select contact point and try to override grouping with the `Disable (...)` option over the existing options.
2. Save
3. Go to Notification policies and save any changes - you will get bad request data
4. create any alert with Select contact point and try to set grouping with the `Disable (...)` option alone.


### Is the bug inside a dashboard panel?

_No response_

### Environment (with versions)?

Grafana: 10.4
OS: Linux
Browser: Chrome


### Grafana platform?

Kubernetes

### Datasource(s)?

_No response_

**What happened**:

Grafana-server Init Failed: Could not find config defaults, make sure homepath command line parameter is set or working directory is homepath

```
brew install grafana
grafana-server
```

**Anything else we need to know?**:

The brew formula gives us the correct line to start:
```
  grafana-server --config=/usr/local/etc/grafana/grafana.ini --homepath /usr/local/share/grafana --packaging=brew cfg:default.paths.logs=/usr/local/var/log/grafana cfg:default.paths.data=/usr/local/var/lib/grafana cfg:default.paths.plugins=/usr/local/var/lib/grafana/plugins
```
But my point is I had a hard time figuring this out with just using the grafana binary itself. 
Worse, I might be sent on a goose chase trying to put a config into my CWD to satisfy the help message.

title: Legal Requirement: Denotative colour coding for Colourblind Users
body:
<!--
Please only use this template for submitting accessibility issues.

This is a new feature area for Grafana that we want to improve. We have long way to go
to really improve accessibility and would like your help to know where to start.
-->

Those who have any difficulties distinguishing colour currently find Grafana difficult to use; when all colours look the same, there is no way to distinguish different series. Grafana should be designed for those who have [Monochromacy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monochromacy), so that it is accessible to everyone.

**Steps to reproduce**:
All graphs use colour alone to distinguish information.

**Actual Result**:
All graphs use colour alone to distinguish information.

**Expected Result**
Color should not be used as the only visual means of conveying information. Graphs should include patterns _and_ colours to better enable all users to distinguish information.

This example is taken from [Trello](https://trello.com/). While Trello is an entirely separate entity, Trello cards can be categorised using patterns _and_ colours.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14872636/70907338-86178a80-2000-11ea-8127-8fa1ea3fa821.png)

**Relevant WCAG Criteria:**

- [Use of Color](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/?versions=2.0#use-of-color): "_Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element._"
- [Colourblind Awareness in Business](http://www.colourblindawareness.org/business/)
- Many countries have legal accessibility requirements. The [UK Health and Safety
Executive](https://www.hse.gov.uk/pubns/web03.pdf), for example, requires that Connotative colour coding is redesigned to Denotative colour coding.

Grafana currently contravenes these.

**Environment**:
- Grafana version: 6.5.1
- Data source type & version: mysql Ver 14.14
- User OS & Browser: Ubuntu & FireFox, Windows & Chrome

**Relates to:**
This issue is related to the following, though they do not go far enough for full colourblind accessibility.
- [13014](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/13014)
- [18041](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/18041)

title: Support color blind single stat thresholds
body:
This is not an issue of something that is broken, but rather something I just realized.

When building a singlestat panel the provided colors for thresholds are red, yellow and red. This makes sense to the vast majority of people, and it should stay this way.

But, I remember once building in a system that had to provide some form of feedback to people to let them know when things weren't working fine. We used colors, like everybody else does, but then one person in the organization approached to me to explain that he was color blind and that what I was doing was basically useless for him, because he saw no difference between them. I was humbled by this simply because I didn't even consider this to be a thing (the blessings of being in the majority side, I guess).

I took a look at the issue tracker and community and failed to find anything about color blind support.

So, I was thinking that grafana could do something really simple to support this: use stripes, solid, or dots, or whatever it makes sense, but have a way of differentiating thresholds by something else than the color itself.

Something as simple as this, with a tick for "enable color blind" would include all this people who we (at least I) don't usually consider.
